description:
Offered: every third semester
In this course, students read and discuss fiction by writers from different parts of the Caribbean to gain insight and broaden their understanding of fiction produced in these Island nations. The course’s focus is on the relationships between culture and society as this fiction is examined against specific historical, cultural and political backgrounds. It also inquires into values associated with questions of identity, family, ideology, etc.
Prerequisite(s): ENGL 1200
Meets General Education Requirement(s): AIA, DP, LI
